summary: Bisphosphonates inhibit osteoclastic bone resorption and do not affect osteoid formation or mineralization significantly. The main therapeutic effect is through reducing bone turnover rates. Agent1 reasoned that among various options provided, only one accurately described how bisphosphonates work clinically—by targeting osteoclasts without altering other aspects like osteogenesis or maturation processes involved with bones' structural integrity maintenance.
